title: silene crassifolia subsp. primuliflora
intro: Silene crassifolia subsp. primuliflora, also known as the white-seeded catchfly, is a plant species belonging to the Caryophyllaceae family, characterized by its white or pink flowers and hairy leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which make it useful in treating wounds and skin irritations. The health benefits of Silene crassifolia subsp. primuliflora include its potential to reduce inflammation, alleviate digestive issues, and exhibit antioxidant properties. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be applied topically to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
